sourceMovie.title: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb (2014) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Shawn Levy's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Christopher Robin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb clicked for you, Christopher Robin (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb?
faq.answer: Try Christopher Robin, The Monkey King, Rugrats Go Wild—each shares DNA with Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb?
faq.answer: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb (2014) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Fantasy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Night at the Museum Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
